choicesItalian: Buongiorno, vorrei due biglietti per l'autobus urbano.
Salve, cerco la stazione dei treni.
Scusi, quanto costa un taxi per il centro?
choicesEnglish: Good morning, I would like two tickets for the city bus.
Hi, I'm looking for the train station.
Excuse me, how much is a taxi to the center?
Listen, where are the coaches?
correctAnswerItalian: Buongiorno, vorrei due biglietti per l'autobus urbano.
correctAnswerEnglish: Good morning, I would like two tickets for the city bus.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Una richiesta precisa e chiara per iniziare l'acquisto.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai chiedendo biglietti per l'autobus di città.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! A precise and clear request to start the purchase.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're asking for city bus tickets.

choicesEnglish: Single or for the entire day?
For today or tomorrow?
One way or return?
First or second class?
correctAnswerItalian: Singoli o per l'intera giornata?
correctAnswerEnglish: Single or for the entire day?

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Il tabaccaio ti chiede quale tipo di biglietto preferisci.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, la scelta è tra biglietto singolo o giornaliero.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! The tobacconist is asking which type of ticket you prefer.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the choice is between a single or daily ticket.

choicesItalian: Prendo due biglietti singoli, per favore.
Mi dia un biglietto per Roma.
choicesEnglish: I'll take two single tickets, please.
I want the monthly pass.
Give me a ticket to Rome.
I'm looking for a seat.
correctAnswerItalian: Prendo due biglietti singoli, per favore.
correctAnswerEnglish: I'll take two single tickets, please.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Hai fatto la tua scelta in modo educato.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai confermando l'acquisto di due biglietti singoli.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! You've made your choice politely.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're confirming the purchase of two single tickets.

choicesEnglish: That's three euros in total.
It costs ten euros each.
Here is your change.
You must pay at the desk.
correctAnswerItalian: Sono tre euro in tutto.
correctAnswerEnglish: That's three euros in total.

feedback.correctItalian: Sì! L'impiegato ti comunica il prezzo totale.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si parla del costo complessivo dell'acquisto.
feedback.correctEnglish: Yes! The clerk is telling you the total price.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's about the overall cost of the purchase.

choicesItalian: Ricordi di convalidarli appena sale a bordo.
Non dimentichi il resto sul banco.
choicesEnglish: Remember to validate them as soon as you get on board.
Don't forget the change on the counter.
You must sign the ticket.
Put it in your wallet.
correctAnswerItalian: Ricordi di convalidarli appena sale a bordo.
correctAnswerEnglish: Remember to validate them as soon as you get on board.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Un consiglio fondamentale per evitare multe.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, l'istruzione riguarda la convalida (o timbratura).
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Fundamental advice to avoid fines.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the instruction concerns validation (or stamping).

choicesItalian: Mi scusi, questo autobus passa per il Colosseo?
Senta, è questa la direzione giusta?
Ehi, dove si ferma il bus?
choicesEnglish: Excuse me, does this bus pass by the Colosseum?
Good morning, where does this train go?
Listen, is this the right direction?
Hey, where does the bus stop?

correctAnswerEnglish: Excuse me, does this bus pass by the Colosseum?

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Chiedi conferma del percorso all'autista.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, cerchi informazioni sulla fermata del Colosseo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! You're asking the driver for confirmation of the route.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're looking for information on the Colosseum stop.

choicesItalian: No, questo va verso la stazione Termini.
Sì, sale pure, ferma proprio davanti.
No, questo è un bus privato.
choicesEnglish: No, this one goes towards Termini station.
Yes, get on, it stops right in front.
Maybe, I need to look at the map.
No, this is a private bus.
correctAnswerItalian: No, questo va verso la stazione Termini.
correctAnswerEnglish: No, this one goes towards Termini station.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! L'autista ti informa che la destinazione è diversa.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, l'autobus è diretto alla stazione Termini.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! The driver informs you that the destination is different.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the bus is headed to Termini station.

choicesItalian: Deve prendere la linea 85 alla fermata opposta.
Prenda il primo bus che passa.
L'85 non passa più da qui.
choicesEnglish: You must take line 85 at the opposite stop.
Go straight for two hundred meters.
Take the first bus that passes.
The 85 doesn't pass here anymore.
correctAnswerItalian: Deve prendere la linea 85 alla fermata opposta.
correctAnswerEnglish: You must take line 85 at the opposite stop.

feedback.correctItalian: Sì! L'indicazione corretta per raggiungere la tua meta.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, ti suggeriscono di cambiare linea e fermata.
feedback.correctEnglish: Yes! The correct direction to reach your destination.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, they suggest you change line and stop.

choicesItalian: Ah, meno male che ho chiesto! Grazie mille.
choicesEnglish: Ah, it's a good thing I asked! Thanks a lot.

Never mind, I'll take a taxi.

correctAnswerEnglish: Ah, it's a good thing I asked! Thanks a lot.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Esprimi sollievo per non aver sbagliato bus.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, sei contento di aver chiesto informazioni.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! You express relief for not having taken the wrong bus.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're glad you asked for information.

choicesItalian: Salve, è possibile fare un abbonamento?
choicesEnglish: Hi, is it possible to get a pass?
Good morning, I would like to buy a car.
Excuse me, do you rent bicycles here?
Can I travel for free?

correctAnswerEnglish: Hi, is it possible to get a pass?

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Chiedi informazioni su un titolo di viaggio a lungo termine.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, la domanda riguarda la possibilità di fare l'abbonamento.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! You're asking for information on a long-term travel ticket.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the question is about the possibility of getting a pass.

choicesItalian: C'è solo il biglietto da dieci corse.

choicesEnglish: We have the weekly transport card.

You must go to the city hall for that.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! L'impiegato propone una soluzione per una settimana.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, ti propongono una carta valida per una settimana.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! The clerk proposes a solution for a week.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, they are proposing a card valid for one week.

choicesItalian: Il prezzo è di dieci euro.
choicesEnglish: It costs twenty-four euros.
The price is ten euros.

It costs too much for me.
correctAnswerItalian: Costa ventiquattro euro.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, il costo indicato è di 24 euro.
feedback.correctEnglish: Yes! Communicating the price of the pass.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the indicated cost is 24 euros.

choicesEnglish: Is it valid for all transport, bus and metro?

correctAnswerEnglish: Is it valid for all transport, bus and metro?

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, vuoi sapere se copre bus e metropolitana.

feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you want to know if it covers bus and metro.

choicesItalian: Sì, per tutti i mezzi nell'area urbana.
Sì, ma deve pagare un extra.
choicesEnglish: Yes, for all means in the urban area.

correctAnswerEnglish: Yes, for all means in the urban area.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Conferma della validità totale all'interno della città.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, la validità è estesa a tutta l'area urbana.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Confirmation of total validity within the city.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the validity is extended to the entire urban area.

choicesItalian: Me ne dia due, per favore.
choicesEnglish: Perfect, I'll take it.

correctAnswerEnglish: Perfect, I'll take it.

feedback.correctItalian: Sì! Decisione finale d'acquisto.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, hai deciso di comprare la carta trasporti.

choicesEnglish: What time does the next 64 pass?

correctAnswerEnglish: What time does the next 64 pass?

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, il tempo di validità è di novanta minuti.

feedback.correctEnglish: Yes! The typical phrase of the controller getting on the bus.

choicesEnglish: Here is my ticket, I just stamped it.

choicesEnglish: I'm sorry, but your ticket is expired.

feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the problem is that the ticket is no longer valid.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, il controllore sta applicando una multa.

feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the controller is issuing a fine.

choicesEnglish: Can I pay the fine immediately?

feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Often paying immediately reduces the fine amount.

choicesItalian: Sì, se paga ora lo sconto è del 50%.

choicesEnglish: Yes, if you pay now the discount is 50%.

feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Incentive for immediate payment.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, they're offering you a discount for paying right away.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, il simbolo per i biglietti è la lettera T.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! The 'T' indicates the tobacco shop in Italy.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the symbol for tickets is the letter T.

choicesItalian: I bambini sotto i sei anni non pagano.
I figli non hanno bisogno del ticket.
Sotto una certa età è gratis.
choicesEnglish: Children under six do not pay.
Little ones travel for free.
Children don't need a ticket.
Under a certain age it's free.
correctAnswerItalian: I bambini sotto i sei anni non pagano.
correctAnswerEnglish: Children under six do not pay.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Informazione utile per le famiglie.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si specifica l'età per la gratuità.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Useful information for families.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the age for free travel is specified.

choicesEnglish: Warning: public transport strike tomorrow.
Tomorrow the buses are stopped.
There is a protest tomorrow.
No traveling tomorrow.
correctAnswerItalian: Attenzione: sciopero dei mezzi domani.
correctAnswerEnglish: Warning: public transport strike tomorrow.

feedback.correctItalian: Sì! Un avviso molto importante e frequente in Italia.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, l'avviso segnala uno sciopero (protesta dei lavoratori).
feedback.correctEnglish: Yes! A very important and frequent notice in Italy.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the notice signals a strike (workers' protest).

choicesItalian: La porta per scendere è quella in mezzo.
choicesEnglish: You get off through the central door.
Exit from the center of the bus.
The door to get off is the one in the middle.
Do not use the front door.
correctAnswerItalian: Si scende dalla porta centrale.
correctAnswerEnglish: You get off through the central door.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Regola di comportamento a bordo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, l'uscita corretta è quella centrale.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Rule of conduct on board.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the correct exit is the central one.
